brands.name: Once Upon A Child
brands.notes: Children's resale retail franchise founded 1984 and franchised since 1992 by Winmark Corporation; all ~430+ stores are franchised (zero company-owned) across the US and Canada, with low reported failure rates (about 3% over the past three years per Vetted Biz) and steady net unit growth.

riskFlags.title: FDD discloses litigation/bankruptcy history
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz's FDD analysis notes that Once Upon A Child discloses lawsuits and/or bankruptcy information in its FDD; prospective buyers should review Items 3 and 4. No major active franchisee litigation surfaced in public searches.

riskFlags.title: Store-level consumer complaints about buy-low pricing
riskFlags.detail: Consumer review sites carry complaints about low buy offers and customer service at individual locations (e.g., Ocoee, FL report). These are customer-facing reputational issues inherent to the resale model rather than franchisee-franchisor disputes.

nationwide.note: Entrepreneur lists franchising opportunities available in all US regions (plus Canada); Winmark actively recruits new Once Upon A Child franchisees.

franchiseFee.note: Standard new-franchisee fee; $15,000 for a second/subsequent store or for existing Winmark-brand franchisees.

adFundPercent.note: Up to 2% of gross sales IF Winmark imposes an advertising fee; currently franchisees pay a $1,500/yr marketing fee plus a 5% of gross sales local-marketing minimum (combined with cooperative advertising).

avgUnitRevenue.note: Average gross sales of 395 franchised stores open 12+ months under same ownership (median $1,127,506); Item 19 also reports 66.77% average gross profit margin before operating expenses.

totalUnits.note: All 430 units franchised, zero company-owned, at end of 2024 (US and Canada); Entrepreneur's 2026 directory lists 439.

yearFranchising.note: Founders sold franchise rights to Winmark (then Grow Biz International) in 1992.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Discounted $15,000 franchise fee for second and subsequent stores.

brands.name: Pickleball Kingdom
brands.notes: Indoor pickleball club franchise founded 2022 and franchising since 2023; the largest indoor pickleball franchise network by awarded locations (350+ awarded as of late 2025), but the open-unit count is far smaller and the system is very young, so unit economics have a short track record.

riskFlags.title: Large gap between awarded and open locations
riskFlags.detail: Pickleball Kingdom touts 350+ awarded locations (Nov 2025 press release), but Entrepreneur's directory lists only 31 open units and a tracking site counted roughly a dozen open clubs as of May 2025. Most of the system is unbuilt pipeline; buyers should verify open-club performance, not awarded counts.
riskFlags.source: PR Newswire (company release) / Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory

riskFlags.title: Very young, capital-intensive concept
riskFlags.detail: Founded 2022, franchising since 2023, with total investment up to roughly $2.26M for large-footprint indoor real estate. Limited operating history exists to validate long-term demand and unit-level returns in the fast-changing pickleball facility market.

riskFlags.title: Aggressive expansion via master franchising and acquisitions
riskFlags.detail: The company is expanding rapidly through statewide multi-unit deals (e.g., all of California), acquisitions of independent clubs (The Pickle Jar, NJ), and international master franchising (India). Rapid scaling can strain franchisor support infrastructure.

nationwide.note: Entrepreneur lists Pickleball Kingdom as seeking franchisees across the United States and internationally; active multi-unit deals announced in CA, NJ, FL, OH, UT, TX in 2025-2026.

franchiseFee.note: Standard single-unit fee; SharpSheets' 2025 FDD analysis matches.

totalInvestmentMin.note: $940,000-$2,257,600 per 2025 FDD; multi-unit development agreement range is $1,030,000-$2,347,600.

totalUnits.note: Open units per Entrepreneur; company separately claims 350+ awarded (sold but mostly not yet open) locations.

multiUnitAvailable.source: SharpSheets 2025 FDD analysis / company expansion announcements
multiUnitAvailable.note: Multi-Unit Development Agreement offered in FDD; multiple multi-unit deals announced.

isGrowing.note: Nine clubs opening by end of 2025 plus five in January 2026; growth measured in openings, though most awarded units remain unopened.

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange (2025 FDD on file)

brands.name: GolfTEC
brands.notes: World's largest golf-instruction brand (founded 1995, 200+ centers worldwide), but it is NOT currently selling new US franchises: its Franchising.com profile states it is not accepting new applicants, and the system is predominantly company-owned with only a handful of US franchised centers. Listed here for reference; availability omitted.

riskFlags.title: Not currently selling US franchises
riskFlags.detail: GolfTEC's Franchising.com profile states 'GolfTEC is not currently accepting new applicants,' and its corporate site has no active franchise-recruitment page. Prospective buyers cannot currently obtain a new US franchise from this brand.

riskFlags.title: Predominantly corporate-owned; conflicting franchised-unit data
riskFlags.detail: Third-party FDD trackers report sharply divergent unit counts (PeerSense: 6 franchised US units; FranchiseOverview: 25 company-owned and 0 franchised, down from ~183 total units in 2021), while the brand claims 200+ corporate and franchised centers worldwide. The US franchised footprint appears minimal and the domestic system has shifted toward corporate ownership.

riskFlags.title: No Item 19 financial performance representation
riskFlags.detail: GolfTEC's 2024 FDD contains no Item 19 earnings disclosure, so there is no franchisor-provided revenue or profit data for franchised centers. No pending litigation or bankruptcy is disclosed in the FDD.

franchiseFee.note: Historical standard fee from the most recent public FDD; new US franchises are not currently being sold.

adFundPercent.note: Up to 4% of gross revenues (plus 0.8% of merchandise revenue, capped annually).

totalUnits.note: Worldwide locations (corporate and franchised, including international) as of August 2024; US franchised count is far smaller and third-party figures conflict (0-6 franchised).

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange / FranchiseOverview (2024 FDD on public registry)

isGrowing.note: Refers to the US franchised system, which has contracted as the brand shifted to corporate ownership; the overall worldwide brand continues to operate 200+ centers.

brands.name: Fairfield by Marriott
brands.notes: Marriott International's select-service hotel flag, founded 1987; 1,153 hotels open in the US and Canada at end of 2023 (1,147 franchised) per the 2024 FDD. A nearly all-franchised, mature system with very high capital requirements typical of new-build hotel franchising; franchise development runs through hotel-development.marriott.com.

riskFlags.title: Parent-company data-breach litigation and regulatory actions
riskFlags.detail: FDD Item 3 discloses extensive litigation tied to the 2018 Starwood reservation-database breach, including the consolidated MDL In re Marriott International Customer Data Security Breach Litigation (MDL No. 19-md-2879), City of Chicago enforcement action, and investigations by all 50 state AGs, the FTC, and foreign regulators (UK ICO, Canada OPC, Australia OAIC).
riskFlags.source: 2024 Fairfield by Marriott Domestic FDD, Item 3

riskFlags.title: Maryland-only arbitration and litigation
riskFlags.detail: The FDD's state-mandated 'Special Risks' section highlights that franchisees must arbitrate certain disputes only in Maryland and litigate non-arbitrable disputes only in Maryland, which may raise dispute costs for out-of-state owners.
riskFlags.source: 2024 Fairfield by Marriott Domestic FDD, Special Risks page

riskFlags.title: 20-year term, not renewable
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur's directory lists the Fairfield franchise agreement as a 20-year term that is not renewable; continuing beyond the term requires a new agreement on then-current terms. Ongoing fees stack beyond the royalty (Program Services Contribution of 3.85% of gross room sales plus fixed per-room charges, Marriott Bonvoy loyalty charges, etc.).

nationwide.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory / Marriott domestic FDD
nationwide.note: Entrepreneur lists franchising opportunities available throughout the United States and internationally; Marriott issues a US-wide domestic FDD for the brand.

franchiseFee.source: 2024 Fairfield by Marriott Domestic FDD, Item 5
franchiseFee.note: New-to-system application fee: $75,000 plus $400 per guestroom over 125 rooms. (Existing-hotel/transfer applications are the greater of $150,000 or $500/room - not the headline figure.)

totalInvestmentMin.source: 2024 Fairfield by Marriott Domestic FDD, cover page / Item 7
totalInvestmentMin.note: Newly constructed prototypical 80-110 room hotel, EXCLUDING real estate, permits/impact fees, insurance, and contingencies.

totalInvestmentMax.source: 2024 Fairfield by Marriott Domestic FDD, cover page / Item 7
totalInvestmentMax.note: Upper end of the 120-150 room prototype range, excluding real estate.

royaltyPercent.source: 2024 Fairfield by Marriott Domestic FDD, Item 6
royaltyPercent.note: 5.5% of gross room sales (temporary reductions possible under development incentive programs).

adFundPercent.source: 2024 Fairfield by Marriott Domestic FDD, Item 6
adFundPercent.note: Marketing Fund contribution of 2.5% of gross room sales, included within a Program Services Contribution of 3.85% of gross room sales plus $7,000/year plus $135 per guestroom/year.

totalUnits.source: 2024 Fairfield by Marriott Domestic FDD, Items 19-20
totalUnits.note: Open and operating in the US and Canada as of Dec 31, 2023: 1,147 franchised + 6 company-managed/leased. Entrepreneur's 2026 directory lists 1,344 units including international.

absenteeOwnership.note: Hotel franchises are typically investor-owned with third-party professional management.

multiUnitAvailable.source: 2024 Fairfield by Marriott Domestic FDD, Item 5 (multi-unit discount table)
multiUnitAvailable.note: FDD provides multi-unit application-fee discounts; many Fairfield owners are multi-hotel groups.

isGrowing.source: 2024 Fairfield by Marriott Domestic FDD, Item 20

fddAvailable.source: Marriott hotel development site (public FDD PDF)

item19Available.source: 2024 Fairfield by Marriott Domestic FDD, Item 19
item19Available.note: Item 19 reports ADR ($131.42), occupancy (69.2%), and RevPAR ($90.91) for 1,068 franchised hotels in 2023 - not whole-dollar average unit revenue, so avgUnitRevenue is omitted.

brands.name: Scenthound
brands.notes: Membership-based dog wellness/grooming franchise founded 2015 (Jupiter, FL); one of the fastest-growing pet franchises with zero reported closures since franchising began and a 400+ unit pipeline across 32 states. Actively selling franchises via scenthoundfranchising.com, though it publishes no explicit state-by-state open-territory list, so availability is omitted pending direct confirmation.

riskFlags.title: High combined ongoing fee load
riskFlags.detail: 6% royalty plus up to 1.5% brand fund plus a required local advertising spend of 5.5% of gross revenue with a $25,000-$35,000 annual minimum - a total ongoing burden around 11.5%+ of revenue, which is high relative to a ~$453K median AUV.

riskFlags.title: License sales outpacing openings; young system
riskFlags.detail: 124 licenses sold in 2025 versus 33 openings, with a 400+ unit pipeline across 32 states; the 2025 FDD Item 19 covers only 71 franchised units open a full 12 months, so most of the system has little operating history.

riskFlags.title: Wide unit-performance dispersion in Item 19
riskFlags.detail: 2025 FDD Item 19: average gross revenue $452,732 with top quartile at $690,195 and bottom quartile at $243,403; only 36 of 71 qualifying units reported net operating income (avg $86,279, 16.2% of revenue), so bottom-quartile economics may be thin after the ~11.5% fee load.

franchiseFee.source: Franchise Chatter 2025 review (2025 FDD) / Entrepreneur directory

totalInvestmentMin.note: $328,099-$549,869 per 2025 FDD; Entrepreneur's 2026 listing shows a similar $322,999-$550,769 range from a newer filing.

adFundPercent.note: Brand fund of up to 1.5% of gross revenue; a separate required LOCAL ad spend of 5.5% of gross revenue ($25K-$35K/yr min/max) applies on top.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Average gross revenue of the 71 franchised 'Scenters' open the full 12 months of 2024 (top quartile $690,195 / bottom quartile $243,403); ~51% of revenue from memberships.

totalUnits.note: Per Entrepreneur's 2026 listing; the 2025 FDD reported 117 franchised + 5 company-owned at end of 2024.

closureCount.note: Zero reported closures since franchising began (Dec 2020 first franchise opening); 5-year closure rate 0.0%.

veteranDiscount.note: $5,000 off first-unit franchise fee for veterans.

multiUnitAvailable.source: Franchise Times (multi-unit deal coverage) / Scenthound franchising site
multiUnitAvailable.note: Heavy multi-unit participation; site advertises single-unit and multi-unit opportunities.

isGrowing.note: Franchised units grew 18 (2022) to 34 (2023) to 117 (2024); ranked #92 on Entrepreneur's 2026 Fastest-Growing Franchises.
